# Definitions
* An automata, denoted $\alpha$, is an infinite series of frames, denoted $\alpha_{k}$ where $k \geq 0$, generated by a function $\Omega$ operating on the initial input frame $\alpha_{0}$. $\alpha_{k}=\Omega(\alpha_{k-1})$.
* A frame, $\alpha_{k}$ has two components: its time-step, given by $k$, and the state it encodes, given by its value.
